projectDescription: Open-source firmware that transforms a Raspberry Pi Pico or ESP32 microcontroller into a fully compliant FIDO2/WebAuthn hardware security key.
keyFeatures: CTAP 2.1 / CTAP 1 and WebAuthn / U2F support
Discoverable credentials (resident keys) with up to 256 stored credentials
ECDSA and EdDSA with SECP256R1, SECP384R1, SECP521R1, SECP256K1, Ed25519 curves
HMAC-Secret, CredProtect, credBlobs, largeBlobKey, and minPinLength extensions
OATH TOTP/HOTP and Yubikey OTP with emulated keyboard interface
Secure Boot and Secure Lock support on RP2350 and ESP32-S3
One-Time Programming (OTP) master key encryption for flash protection
Enterprise attestation and PIN UV Auth Token with permissions
24-word mnemonic backup and device recovery rescue interface
Compatible with Yubico Authenticator, YKMAN, Nitrokey nitropy/nitroapp
publicApiSurface: CTAP_GET_ASSERTION (0x02) — authenticate with a stored credential
CTAP_CLIENT_PIN (0x06) — set, change, or verify device PIN
CTAP_CONFIG (0x0D) — authenticator configuration (enterprise attestation, PIN policy, LED)
CTAP1 REGISTER (0x01) / AUTHENTICATE (0x02) — legacy U2F commands
OATH AID (A000000527210101) — TOTP/HOTP credential management via CCID
